What's in a name?-Karnataka

# What's in a name?-Karnataka

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Dec 13, 2022

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

While most of us know about Bengaluru getting it’s name from Bendakaluru meaning village of boiled beans and Mysuru being named after Mahishasura, how many know about the name origins of other places in Karnataka.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!a-mF!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F5d9654d9-d4bf-4d88-bc71-1124a6cbc4b1_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!a-mF!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F5d9654d9-d4bf-4d88-bc71-1124a6cbc4b1_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R-pp!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2804de3d-9b83-4d61-b8cc-38c9d1f6d4a9_1200x700.jpeg)

![THE 30 BEST Places to Visit in Bagalkot District (2025)](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R-pp!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2804de3d-9b83-4d61-b8cc-38c9d1f6d4a9_1200x700.jpeg "THE 30 BEST Places to Visit in Bagalkot District (2025)")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ZY5S!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff1d1278c-6e39-4f5c-8a61-df5e651f5362_800x542.jpeg)

![Reconstructed Shirur Agasi](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ZY5S!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff1d1278c-6e39-4f5c-8a61-df5e651f5362_800x542.jpeg "Reconstructed Shirur Agasi")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Bagalkot’s older name was Bagadige as per inscriptions, it’s believed the area was given by Ravan to his court musicians as a grant. As per historical records, the town was given by Bijapur ruler Ibrahim Adil Shah II, as bangle money to his daughter, a tradition by which girls are given money by parents to buy bangles, gold, ornaments.

History Under Your Feet is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

Subscribe

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!OD6L!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fcfe13d5d-d64c-4d23-93f2-58f765d8ec64_1600x1067.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!OD6L!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fcfe13d5d-d64c-4d23-93f2-58f765d8ec64_1600x1067.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Incidentally names of other places in Bagalkot district, too have Pauranic origins. Badami aka Vatapi, the capital of the Chalukyas was named after an Asura ruler, who was vanquished by Sage Agastya. Another place in the district Jamkhandi, gets it’s name from the temple to Jambukeswara here. Aihole famous for it’s monuments and temples, was earlier called Ayyavole meaning noble city, while another heritage site Pattadakal was previously called Raktapura, it’s name means Coronation Place, and was one of the major centres during the Chalukyan era.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0cPP!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd299dc43-65b2-4a19-8dc8-6e2809a3020d_1920x746.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0cPP!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd299dc43-65b2-4a19-8dc8-6e2809a3020d_1920x746.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Belagavi was originally called Venugrama meaning Village of Bamboos, that in due course became corrupted to Belagavi and later Belgaum during British era. Chikodi was so called as it was believed to be a smaller village( Chikka Kodi) compared to it’s neighbouring Hirekudi. The town of Gokak get’s it’s name from the Goki trees here, as also the fact that many cow herders settled here. Haveri gets it’s name from two words Havu(snake) and Keri( lake).

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!5UB0!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fbe5ef850-5c2b-42a3-8f61-5111c140dc74_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!5UB0!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fbe5ef850-5c2b-42a3-8f61-5111c140dc74_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!_QkP!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fcec3ff56-9c43-48df-8190-5ef6cc143df2_3264x2448.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!_QkP!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fcec3ff56-9c43-48df-8190-5ef6cc143df2_3264x2448.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Dharwad’s earlier name was Dwarawada from two words Dwara(Door) and Wada(town), so called as it was a resting place for travellers, as well as being the gateway to Malenadu, while it’s twin city Hubbali gets it’s name from a Kannad word Hoovina Balli meaning “Flowering Creeper”.

Hospete the gateway to Hampi, was built by Sri Krishnadeva Raya in 1520 in honor of his mother Nagalambika and named Nagalapura. However people referred to it as Hosa Pete meaning New City. Incidentally area between Hampi and Hospet is still called as Nagulapura.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!RybA!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F90cc342c-70a4-4986-8393-0578e960a22b_800x537.jpeg)

![Ballari city](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!RybA!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F90cc342c-70a4-4986-8393-0578e960a22b_800x537.jpeg "Ballari city")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Ballari is believed to have got it’s name from a Shiva bhakta Ballesha Malayya, who had the desire to see Mallesha. It’s believed Shiva revealed himself in a Balla(grain), and hence the name.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WV9H!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F79e1e2f9-4892-4d28-a0f3-e0861dbc4509_1024x640.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WV9H!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F79e1e2f9-4892-4d28-a0f3-e0861dbc4509_1024x640.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Bidar one of the Bahmani kingdoms, was called as Viduranagara in ancient times, as it’s believed Vidura, the wise minister of the Kauravas lived here. It’s also believed to be the place where Nala and Damayanti met. During Bahmani rule this was called as Muhammadabad. Basavakalyan’s older name was Kalyani, the capital of the Western Chalukyas, it was later renamed in honor of Basaveshwara the Lingayat saint of the Bhakti movement.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!gDS1!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F99a16149-0823-40f6-9940-d4ff4af3d774_800x533.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!gDS1!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F99a16149-0823-40f6-9940-d4ff4af3d774_800x533.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Bijapur famous for the Gol Gumbaz, was originally called as Vijayapura, and the foundation here was laid by the Chalukyas of Kalyani between the 10th and 11th centuries. It was the largest of the Bahmani kingdoms ruled by the Adil Shahis, before falling to Aurangzeb in 1686.

Kalaburagi gets it’s name from a Kannada term meaning Land of Stones and Thorns, due to the rocky landscape here. The Bahmani Sultanate was founded here by Alauddin Bahman Shah, and it was called as Gulbarga, Urdu for city of flowers and gardens.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!T4CN!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa82d7f04-0504-4fa1-b805-5083707471e1_700x394.jpeg)

![Chamarajeshwara temple](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!T4CN!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa82d7f04-0504-4fa1-b805-5083707471e1_700x394.jpeg "Chamarajeshwara temple")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vJZy!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3bbc04de-bef1-497c-bc44-7e35ded30ba3_1024x576.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vJZy!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3bbc04de-bef1-497c-bc44-7e35ded30ba3_1024x576.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Chamarajanagara, was originally called Sri Arikottara,it gets it’s current name from Chamaraja Wodeyar who was born here. Chikaballapura gets it’s name from a local term meaning “Town(Pura) where grain(Balla) is measured in small quantities(Chika)”.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!htzn!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffc2bc8cb-26c4-42dd-a122-47dfdd1bae27_799x600.jpeg)

![Hasanamba Devi Photo](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!htzn!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffc2bc8cb-26c4-42dd-a122-47dfdd1bae27_799x600.jpeg "Hasanamba Devi Photo")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!sVb0!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F65e56d74-2a7a-4220-bfd8-9fbb4dadbedf_800x588.jpeg)

![Shravanabela viewed from a hill](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!sVb0!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F65e56d74-2a7a-4220-bfd8-9fbb4dadbedf_800x588.jpeg "Shravanabela viewed from a hill")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Hassan is named after the presiding deity Haasanamba whose temple is located in the town. Shravanabelagola, a prominent Jaina pilgrimage center, literal meaning is “White pond of the Shravana”, referring to the pond in the middle of the town.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!p5gc!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F595444c4-de61-4a2f-9582-ecaf8bfac8d4_600x400.jpeg)

![Grasslands in Kumara Parvatha, Madikeri during Monsoon season](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!p5gc!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F595444c4-de61-4a2f-9582-ecaf8bfac8d4_600x400.jpeg "Grasslands in Kumara Parvatha, Madikeri during Monsoon season")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Madikeri, the HQ of Kodagu gets it’s name from Muddu Raja Keri, after the Haleri ruler Mudduraja, while another town in Kodagu, Virajpet is named after the Haleri ruler Dodda Virarajendra who founded this place.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!qeaU!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd7fd6595-a6ae-4853-8794-171998009fba_1024x576.jpeg)

![Sri Vidyashankara temple (1342 CE) at Sringeri](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!qeaU!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd7fd6595-a6ae-4853-8794-171998009fba_1024x576.jpeg "Sri Vidyashankara temple (1342 CE) at Sringeri")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!VdAE!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe01daac0-e448-40fb-b234-d532a0b12177_800x533.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!VdAE!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe01daac0-e448-40fb-b234-d532a0b12177_800x533.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Chikamagaluru literally means Town of Younger Daughter, as it was believed to have been given in dowry to the younger daughter of the king Rukmangada. The holy town of Shringeri, gets it’s name from the Rishyasringa Giri, the abode of the sage Rishyasringa, whose presence bought rains to the drought hit Romapada kingdom.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HOG5!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F07e6dcfe-d07b-4a41-b01c-2a6614d7d3d3_369x246.jpeg)

![Someshwara Temple](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HOG5!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F07e6dcfe-d07b-4a41-b01c-2a6614d7d3d3_369x246.jpeg "Someshwara Temple")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Kolar’s name is derived from Kolahalapura, meaning city of conflict, as it was the site of many battles between the Chalukyas and Cholas. The Kolaramma Temple dedicated to the presiding deity was built by the Cholas.

Mandya is named after Rishi Mandavya who is believed to have meditated here. It was also called as Vedaranya in ancient times, as well as Vishnupura. Srirangapatnam gets it’s name from the Ranganatha Temple here, and it is one of the Pancharanga Kshetras on the Kaveri, others being Shivanasamudram in Karnataka, Srirangam, Kovilvadi and Parimala Ranganatha temple in Mayildathurai district.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!IOHt!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff30cbc52-0c33-42df-8dec-5cad3941210b_800x1067.jpeg)

![Ranganathaswamy Temple, Srirangapatna](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!IOHt!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff30cbc52-0c33-42df-8dec-5cad3941210b_800x1067.jpeg "Ranganathaswamy Temple, Srirangapatna")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vj8R!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F110bf183-3627-4652-a609-0e93eede3698_800x530.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vj8R!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F110bf183-3627-4652-a609-0e93eede3698_800x530.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Tumkur ‘s name comes from Tumbe Ooru, due to the abundance of the Tumbe Hoovu, a kind of flower here. Was also called as Kalpatharu Nadu due to abundance of coconut trees here. Another version states that the guards here used to alert people with tumaki, a kind of drum, hence the name.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!6-UL!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6e6fea09-1dfe-40b2-8d00-c339c09c7371_800x533.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!6-UL!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6e6fea09-1dfe-40b2-8d00-c339c09c7371_800x533.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4qR9!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7f217361-2ce2-42f8-ae77-58736405f59e_640x426.jpeg)

![Another view of Fort](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4qR9!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7f217361-2ce2-42f8-ae77-58736405f59e_640x426.jpeg "Another view of Fort")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Chitradurga gets it’s name from Chitrakaladurga,meaning a picturesque fort, owing to the umbrella shaped hill near it, where the Fort was located. Was also called as Farrukhabad during Tipu Sultan’s rule.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!fEGK!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6fab3ddf-96f4-4ba6-aeaf-6f6712f83f9a_800x533.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!fEGK!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6fab3ddf-96f4-4ba6-aeaf-6f6712f83f9a_800x533.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Shivamogga is so called as it’s believed Shiva drank the waters of the Tunga river here in a pot(Mogge), while another version states it comes from a term Sihi Mogge(sweet pot).

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!j6HD!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fad8f9dd1-5443-4d61-9c32-b4c6be0fdf83_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!j6HD!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fad8f9dd1-5443-4d61-9c32-b4c6be0fdf83_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Davanagere literally means “village of lakes”. It’s believed that the Chalukyan rulers once travelling here, decided to build a small tank to quench their thirst, which is how it got it’s name. Carved out of Shimoga, Tumkur and Chitradurga districts, this city was called Manchester of Karnataka due to large number of textile factories here.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!x5e1!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F71b7cdd8-b921-43aa-a2bf-7169bf9244c8_1024x649.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!x5e1!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F71b7cdd8-b921-43aa-a2bf-7169bf9244c8_1024x649.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Mangaluru is named after the presiding deity Mangala Devi, it’s believed a local princess Parimala renounced her kingdom and became a disciple of Matsyendranath, who renamed her as Mangala Devi. Moodabidri famous for the Jain temple here, gets it’s name from two words Mudu( East) and Bidri( Bamboo) due to the bamboo crop here.

Udupi gets it’s name from a Tulu word “Odipu” meaning emergence, while some state that it also gets it’s name from Udupa,a Sanskrit word for moon. The town is famous for the Krishna temple here.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!uN0j!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F425d01e8-bd0e-4c41-9911-1289e7eda40d_800x536.jpeg)

![Madhva sarovara of Udupi Sri Krishna Temple](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!uN0j!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F425d01e8-bd0e-4c41-9911-1289e7eda40d_800x536.jpeg "Madhva sarovara of Udupi Sri Krishna Temple")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!RYDO!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6c998267-db1c-4978-be5e-bb2be4ada3e1_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!RYDO!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6c998267-db1c-4978-be5e-bb2be4ada3e1_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Karwar gets it’s name from Kadwad, meaning the last precinct in Konkani, it’s older name was Baithkol meaning bay of safety.
